title: Function-like macros should not be invoked without all of their arguments
description: Function-like macros should not be invoked without all of their argumentsAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugcwebased-on-misrapreprocessorWhy is this an issue?More InfoBefore compilation, the preprocessor replaces macros with the code they expand to. The resulting code may be invalid when a function-like macro is invoked without all its arguments, which can cause the program to fail to compile or to behave unexpectedly at runtime.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since9.1©2025SonarSource Sàrl. All rights reserved.Privacy Policy|Cookie Policy|Terms of Use
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
#define ADD(a, b) ((a) + (b))
int result = ADD(5);  // Noncompliant: missing second argument

#define LOG(level, msg) printf("%s: %s\n", level, msg)
LOG("ERROR");  // Noncompliant: missing msg argument
compliant:
#define ADD(a, b) ((a) + (b))
int result = ADD(5, 3);  // Compliant: all arguments provided

#define LOG(level, msg) printf("%s: %s\n", level, msg)
LOG("ERROR", "File not found");  // Compliant: both arguments provided

checks.message: Function-like macro called with insufficient arguments
